description: A polymorphic Foundation CSS button element that can render as an anchor, button, or input, with optional style modifiers including primary, success, menu, and radius variants.

stories.name: MenuToggle
stories.description: Mobile navigation menu toggle anchor with burger icon, the most common usage site-wide

stories.name: PrimaryButton
stories.description: Primary style button element for form submission

notes: The .button class is a Foundation CSS framework button class used polymorphically across the site. The dominant usage (26258 pages) is as a mobile menu toggle anchor with class 'button menu' containing a decorative span.section-title-burger for the hamburger icon. The 'as' prop controls which element is rendered. For input type='submit', the label maps to the value attribute. The section-title-burger span is purely decorative (aria-hidden) and rendered when showBurgerIcon is true.
